prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_integration_subject producer_instance_1)
(register_integration_subject consumer_instance_1)
(register_integration_subject integration_coordinator_1)
(register_integration_subject integration_coordinator_2)
(assign_adapter_to_subject producer_instance_1 adapter_endpoint_1)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(confirm_subject_verification producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(attach_configuration_to_producer producer_instance_1 cache_cluster_1 configuration_artifact_1)
(finalize_producer_priming producer_instance_1 cache_cluster_1 verification_token_1 configuration_artifact_1)
(release_verification_token producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(assign_adapter_to_subject consumer_instance_1 adapter_endpoint_2)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject consumer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(confirm_subject_verification consumer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(attach_configuration_to_consumer consumer_instance_1 cache_shard_1 configuration_artifact_1)
(finalize_consumer_priming consumer_instance_1 cache_shard_1 verification_token_1 configuration_artifact_1)
(release_verification_token consumer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(assemble_deployment_artifact producer_instance_1 consumer_instance_1 cache_cluster_1 cache_shard_1 deployment_artifact_1)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(prepare_deployment_artifact deployment_artifact_1 producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(mark_producer_ready_for_commit producer_instance_1 deployment_artifact_1)
(mark_consumer_ready_for_commit consumer_instance_1 deployment_artifact_1)
(release_verification_token producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(assign_adapter_to_subject integration_coordinator_1 adapter_endpoint_3)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject integration_coordinator_1 verification_token_1)
(confirm_subject_verification integration_coordinator_1 verification_token_1)
(consume_and_bind_secret integration_coordinator_1 secret_material_1 deployment_artifact_1)
(mark_coordinator_artifact_secured integration_coordinator_1 secret_material_1 deployment_artifact_1 verification_token_1)
(release_verification_token integration_coordinator_1 verification_token_1)
(claim_runtime_credential_for_coordinator integration_coordinator_1 runtime_credential_1)
(claim_commit_token_for_coordinator integration_coordinator_1 commit_token_1)
(attach_operator_action_to_subject integration_coordinator_1 operator_action_1)
(initiate_coordinator_verification_no_producer_variant integration_coordinator_1 runtime_credential_1 operator_action_1 secret_material_1 deployment_artifact_1)
(finalize_coordinator_verification_with_commit_token integration_coordinator_1 commit_token_1 secret_material_1 deployment_artifact_1)
(promote_coordinator_to_committed integration_coordinator_1)
(release_runtime_credential_from_coordinator integration_coordinator_1 runtime_credential_1)
(release_commit_token_from_coordinator integration_coordinator_1 commit_token_1)
(detach_operator_action_from_subject integration_coordinator_1 operator_action_1)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(bind_environment_and_mark_subject_precommit producer_instance_1 environment_context_1 verification_token_1)
(finalize_subject_commit_from_producer producer_instance_1 adapter_endpoint_1 environment_context_1)
(release_verification_token producer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(assign_adapter_to_subject integration_coordinator_2 adapter_endpoint_1)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject integration_coordinator_2 verification_token_1)
(confirm_subject_verification integration_coordinator_2 verification_token_1)
(consume_and_bind_secret integration_coordinator_2 secret_material_2 deployment_artifact_1)
(mark_coordinator_artifact_secured integration_coordinator_2 secret_material_2 deployment_artifact_1 verification_token_1)
(release_verification_token integration_coordinator_2 verification_token_1)
(claim_runtime_credential_for_coordinator integration_coordinator_2 runtime_credential_1)
(claim_commit_token_for_coordinator integration_coordinator_2 commit_token_1)
(attach_operator_action_to_subject integration_coordinator_2 operator_action_1)
(initiate_coordinator_verification_no_producer_variant integration_coordinator_2 runtime_credential_1 operator_action_1 secret_material_2 deployment_artifact_1)
(finalize_coordinator_verification_with_commit_token integration_coordinator_2 commit_token_1 secret_material_2 deployment_artifact_1)
(promote_coordinator_to_committed integration_coordinator_2)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject consumer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(bind_environment_and_mark_subject_precommit consumer_instance_1 environment_context_1 verification_token_1)
(finalize_subject_commit_from_consumer consumer_instance_1 adapter_endpoint_2 environment_context_1)
(release_verification_token consumer_instance_1 verification_token_1)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject integration_coordinator_1 verification_token_1)
(bind_environment_and_mark_subject_precommit integration_coordinator_1 environment_context_1 verification_token_1)
(finalize_subject_commit_from_coordinator integration_coordinator_1 adapter_endpoint_3 environment_context_1)
(release_verification_token integration_coordinator_1 verification_token_1)
(bind_verification_token_to_subject integration_coordinator_2 verification_token_1)
(bind_environment_and_mark_subject_precommit integration_coordinator_2 environment_context_1 verification_token_1)
(finalize_subject_commit_from_coordinator integration_coordinator_2 adapter_endpoint_1 environment_context_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a distributed cache integration scenario with producer_instance_1, consumer_instance_1, and two integration_coordinators: integration_coordinator_1 and _2. The goal is to commit all four subjects: producer, consumer, and both coordinators.


## Examining Distributed Cache Integration

- The scenario involves integrating a distributed cache with four key components: producer_instance_1, consumer_instance_1, and two integration_coordinators (integration_coordinator_1 and _2).
- The objective is to ensure all four subjects—producer, consumer, and both coordinators—are successfully committed.

## Examining Distributed Cache Integration
- All subjects—producer, consumer, and both coordinators—must be registered, assigned adapters, and verified to achieve commitment.
- Committing requires binding a verification token, confirming verification, and reaching ready_for_commit status.

## Checking Resource Availability
- Only one environment_context_1, verification_token_1, commit_token_1, and configuration_artifact_1 are available, shared across subjects.
- Resources like adapters and environment contexts are consumed during binding but released after finalizing the commit, allowing sequential use.

## Exploring Commit Sequencing
- Producers and consumers must prime caches and assemble artifacts, which are currently pending, before reaching ready_for_commit.
- Coordinators have multiple paths to ready_for_commit, but all subjects need to bind the single environment context sequentially to finalize commitment.

## Exploring Commit Paths  
- For producers and consumers, two paths lead to ready_for_commit: one starts with attaching configuration, setting config_applied and processing, then finalizing priming to reach primed and ready states.  
- The other path primes first, applies an operator to set processing and ready, but leaves config_applied false, affecting later assembly variants.  

## Checking Assembly Requirements  
- Assembly of the deployment artifact requires cache_cluster_primed and cache_shard_primed, with readiness for both producer and consumer, but not necessarily config_applied.  
- Different variants (producer, consumer, both) have specific requirements, like cache_cluster_configuration_applied for consumer_variant, limiting flexibility based on state.  

## Managing Resource Constraints  
- Only one configuration_artifact_1 and operator_action_1 are available, requiring careful sequencing to meet variant needs.  
- Verification uses a single verification_token_1, bound and released sequentially to verify each subject, ensuring all can reach verified status despite the limitation.

## Planning Subject Verification  
- All subjects, including producer, consumer, and both coordinators, must be registered and assigned adapters before verification.  
- Use the single verification_token_1 sequentially, binding and confirming verification for each subject, starting with the producer.  

## Sequencing Configuration Attachment  
- Attach configuration to the producer first using configuration_artifact_1, setting config_applied and processing states, then finalize priming to make it ready.  
- After finalizing, the configuration artifact becomes available again, allowing attachment to the consumer in a similar sequence.  

## Checking Priming and Readiness  
- The config path for priming sets primed and ready states during finalize_producer_priming, without needing prime_cache, and keeps config_applied true.  
- Both producer and consumer need to be primed and ready for full assembly, ensuring cache_cluster_1 and cache_shard_1 are configured appropriately.

## Extending Coordination Readiness  
- Coordinators (coord1, coord2) need registration, adapter assignment, and verification, using the now-available verification_token_1.  
- After verifying, coordinators follow coordinator paths, requiring artifact assembly and secret consumption to reach ready_for_commit.  

## Handling Coordinator Paths  
- Coordinator paths involve securing artifacts via mark_coordinator_artifact_secured, requiring verification and no consumer-only variant, which fits the full assembly case.  
- Post-securing, initiate_coordinator_verification_no_producer_variant leads to verification_ready, using subject_operator_action_binding and bound secrets.  

## Finalizing Coordinator Commitment  
- After reaching verification_ready, finalize_coordinator_verification_with_commit_token sets a milestone, requiring commit_token_binding and no variant restrictions.  
- Promote_coordinator_to_committed then marks the coordinator committed and ready_for_commit, essential for later execution phases.

## Preparing Producer and Consumer for Commit  
- Producer and consumer can reach ready_for_commit without coordinator involvement, needing only artifact assembly and preparation.  
- Their process involves assembling, preparing artifacts, and marking them ready, distinct from coordinator paths.

## Managing Coordinator Verification  
- Coordinators require subject_operator_action_binding and runtime_credential_binding to progress through verification, using shared resources like runtime and commit_token.  
- The promote_to_committed path enables coordinators to reach ready_for_commit, requiring verified status, secret binding, and specific conditions like no variants.

## Handling Resource Constraints  
- With shared resources (one runtime, commit_token, operator_action, verification_token), coordinators must process sequentially, as bindings remain after actions.  
- Releasing resources (e.g., release_runtime_credential_from_coordinator) is needed for the second coordinator, with no preconditions against committed status.

## Continuing Resource Management  
- After promoting a coordinator to committed, release runtime, commit token, and operator action from the first coordinator, making them available for the next.  
- This sequential release enables the second coordinator to claim resources, ensuring no resource conflicts in the process.  

## Sequencing Coordinator Verification  
- For the second coordinator, consume and bind a new secret after verification, using the now-available verification token from the first coordinator.  
- After marking the second coordinator as secured, release the verification token, then proceed with runtime and operator claims to finalize verification.  

## Finalizing Multiple Coordinators  
- The process repeats for each coordinator, ensuring each reaches ready_for_commit without variant restrictions, using shared resources sequentially.  
- Final promotion to committed requires milestone achievement, with no verified status needed, maintaining consistency across coordinators.

## Continuing Consumer and Producer Processes  
- Confirming consumer1 with token1 marks it as verified, enabling further configuration steps.  
- Attaching cache_shard_1 to consumer1 applies configuration, binding it and making config unavailable until finalized.  

## Finalizing Consumer Readiness  
- Finalizing consumer1 priming with token1 and config1 primes the shard, making consumer ready and config available again.  
- Releasing the verification token from consumer1 makes it available for other processes, ensuring no binding conflicts.  

## Preparing Deployment Artifacts  
- Assembling deployment_artifact_1 involves binding producer1 and consumer1 with cache_cluster_1 and cache_shard_1, marking it as not pending.  
- Binding verification to producer1 with token1, after ensuring adapter assignment, prepares the deployment artifact for further steps.  

## Managing Coordinator Integration  
- Registering and assigning adapter_endpoint_3 to integration_coordinator_1 sets up coordinator verification, requiring token availability.  
- Releasing the verification token from producer1 first ensures it’s available for binding to coord1, enabling verification confirmation.  

## Advancing Coordinator Verification  
- Consuming and binding secret_material_1 to coord1 with deployment_artifact_1 marks the secret as consumed and bound, no longer available.  
- Marking coord1 as secured requires verified status, secret claims, and verification binding, with no consumer variant, leading to token release.

## Committing Consumer Instance  
- Binding verification_token_1 to consumer_instance_1 starts the commit process, with environment_context_1 bound and marked as unavailable.  
- Finalizing consumer_instance_1 commit releases adapter_endpoint_2 and environment_context_1, marking the consumer as committed and the token still bound.  

## Releasing Tokens for Coordinators  
- Releasing the verification token from consumer_instance_1 makes it available for other processes, avoiding binding conflicts.  
- Binding verification_token_1 to integration_coordinator_1 begins its commit process, similar to the consumer, with environment_context_1.  

## Finalizing Coordinator Commitments  
- Committing integration_coordinator_1 releases adapter_endpoint_3 and environment_context_1, marking it as committed and freeing the token.  
- Repeating the process, integration_coordinator_2 commits with adapter_endpoint_1, ensuring all components are finalized and resources available.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
